revision.text:
[[category:new pages]] '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site.
Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵).
Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018).</ref> Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Wō (King of Wa)|ja|武 (倭王)}}.
== Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014">{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}</ref> Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum.
The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳1989">{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}</ref> Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
== Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.<ref name="千田2017年">{{Harvid|千田2017年}}</ref>
== Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
== Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 150 meters * Height: 20 meters * Front square section: * Width: 180 meters * Height: 16.6 meters
This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide.<ref name="堺市" />
== Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound).<ref name="鉢塚古墳と割塚古墳" />
== Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture
'''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Wō (King of Wa)|ja|武 (倭王)}}

The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.<ref name="千田2017年">{{Harvid|千田2017年}}</ref>

== Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 150 meters * Height: 20 meters * Front square section: * Width: 180 meters * Height: 16.6 meters
This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide.<ref name="堺市" />
== Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound).<ref name="鉢塚古墳と割塚古墳" />

The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳1989">{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}</ref> Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
== Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.<ref name="千田2017年">{{Harvid|千田2017年}}</ref>
== Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
== Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 150 meters * Height: 20 meters * Front square section: * Width: 180 meters * Height: 16.6 meters
This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide.<ref name="堺市" />
== Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound).<ref name="鉢塚古墳と割塚古墳" />
== Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture
'''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}

[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site.
Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵).
Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018).</ref> Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}.

== Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 150 meters * Height: 20 meters * Front square section: * Width: 180 meters * Height: 16.6 meters
This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide.<ref name="堺市" />

== Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
== Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 150 meters * Height: 20 meters * Front square section: * Width: 180 meters * Height: 16.6 meters
This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide.<ref name="堺市" />
== Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound).<ref name="鉢塚古墳と割塚古墳" />
== Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture
'''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}
[[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site.
Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵).
Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018).</ref> Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}.
== Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014">{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}</ref> Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum.
The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳1989">{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}</ref> Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
== Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.<ref name="千田2017年">{{Harvid|千田2017年}}</ref>
== Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu.<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" />
== Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows:<ref name="仲哀天皇陵古墳2014" /> * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 150 meters * Height: 20 meters * Front square section: * Width: 180 meters * Height: 16.6 meters
This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide.<ref name="堺市" />
== Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound).<ref name="鉢塚古墳と割塚古墳" />
== Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture
'''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}

The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 150 meters * Height: 20 meters * Front square section: * Width: 180 meters * Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Japanese Kofun |Name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |BelongsTo = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |LatitudeDegrees = 34|LatitudeMinutes = 33|LatitudeSeconds = 55.35 |LongitudeDegrees = 135|LongitudeMinutes = 35|LongitudeSeconds = 38.60 |ISO = JP-27 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:Images]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:images]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''Location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}

(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]]

(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |BelongsTo = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |LatitudeDegrees = 34|LatitudeMinutes = 33|LatitudeSeconds = 55.35 |LongitudeDegrees = 135|LongitudeMinutes = 35|LongitudeSeconds = 38.60 |ISO = JP-27 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}

(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |BelongsTo = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |LatitudeDegrees = 34|LatitudeMinutes = 33|LatitudeSeconds = 55.35 |LongitudeDegrees = 135|LongitudeMinutes = 35|LongitudeSeconds = 38.60 |ISO = JP-27 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Dimensions = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}) and one of the burial mounds comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest burial mound.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages
