Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017年}} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}

(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun Size Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |Map = Osaka city |Label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |LabelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb Size Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Dimensions == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |BelongsTo = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |LatitudeDegrees = 34|LatitudeMinutes = 33|LatitudeSeconds = 55.35 |LongitudeDegrees = 135|LongitudeMinutes = 35|LongitudeSeconds = 38.60 |ISO = JP-27 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_labelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_labelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Cultural Heritage site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_labelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun part_of in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the shape of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun part_of and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun part_of]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_labelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_labelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |BelongsTo = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |coordinatesDegrees = 34|coordinatesMinutes = 33|coordinatesSeconds = 55.35 |coordinatesDegrees = 135|coordinatesMinutes = 35|coordinatesSeconds = 38.60 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |ConstructionPeriod = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |BurialFacility = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |Notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_labelPosition = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:kamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designated as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |BelongsTo = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 33|coordinatesonds = 55.35 |coordinatesrees = 135|coordinatesutes = 35|coordinatesonds = 38.60 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}

Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |BelongsTo = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 33|coordinatesonds = 55.35 |coordinatesrees = 135|coordinatesutes = 35|coordinatesonds = 38.60 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) |AccompanyingMounds = 1 |BurialPerson = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, figurative haniwa |ImperialTomb = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[sn:Okamisanzai The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == Burial Facilities == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World other_designation site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the
