But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}, Fujidera 4-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 55.35 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 38.60 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) |notes = 1 |Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, figurative haniwa |Mausoleum = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:kamisanzai Kofun]]

revision.comment: 74 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳 up to rev 99994837
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 岡ミサンザイ古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属 = [[古市古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺4丁目 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 55.35 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 35|経度秒 = 38.60 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長245m
高さ20m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末葉 |埋葬施設 = 不明（一説に[[横穴式石室]]） |陪塚 = 1基 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第14代[[仲哀天皇]]
（一説）第21代[[雄略天皇]]
（一説）[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]] |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・形象埴輪 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「恵我長野西陵」 |特記事項 = 全国第16位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |地図 = Osaka city |ラベル = 岡
ミサンザイ
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南西 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, haisho-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[仲哀天皇]]恵我長野西陵 拝所}}]] '''岡ミサンザイ古墳'''（おかみさんざいこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''恵我長野西陵'''（えがのながののにしのみささぎ、惠我長野西陵）」として第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 全国では第16位の規模の古墳で、[[5世紀]]末葉（[[古墳時代]]中期）頃の築造と推定される。考古学には第21代[[雄略天皇]]、[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]]（[[倭の五王]]の1人）の真陵とする説が有力視される。 == 概要 == 大阪府東部、藤井寺市・[[羽曳野市]]・[[松原市]]にまたがる大古墳群である[[古市古墳群]]のうち、西端の位置に築造された巨大前方後円墳である。古墳名の「岡」は地名で、「ミサンザイ」は「ミササギ（陵）」の転訛。墳丘は[[中世]]の山城築城、および幕末の修陵の際に大きく改変を受けている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の[[仲哀天皇]]陵として同庁の管理下にあるが、これまでに墳丘裾部や古墳周辺部において[[宮内庁書陵部]]や大阪府教育委員会・藤井寺市教育委員会による数次の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南南西方に向ける{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は元は3段築成と見られるが、修陵の際に5段に改変されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘外表では[[埴輪]]片が検出されている一方、[[葺石]]は認められていない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘くびれ部東側には[[造出]]を有する（西側造出の存在有無は不明）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘周囲には幅広の周濠（主軸線上で幅50メートル）が巡らされるほか、その外側には埴輪列を有する周堤（幅20メートル）が巡らされる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、墳丘形状から[[横穴式石室]]である可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。副葬品は不明{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。また周辺では、後円部北側に[[陪塚]]と見られる鉢塚古墳（国の[[史跡]]）が築造されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 この岡ミサンザイ古墳は、出土埴輪より[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。築造当時としては全国で突出する規模であり、葺石の停止および横穴式石室の新規導入（推定）の点で、それまでの古墳とは一線を画す様相の古墳になる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、前述のように現在は宮内庁により第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の陵に治定されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。ただし仲哀天皇は非実在性が強く、築造年代も仲哀天皇の想定年代に合わないことから、現在では第21代[[雄略天皇]]や、同天皇と同一視される[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]]（『[[宋書]]』倭国伝）・ワカタケル大王（[[稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣]]銘）の陵とする説が有力視される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）、[[戸田忠至|戸田大和守]]により[[仲哀天皇]]の陵に治定{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）-[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）、修陵{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[明治]]初年、墳丘上の雑木林（クヌギなど）の伐採、マツ・スギの植林{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * 明治期、宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により仲哀天皇陵に治定。 * 明治期、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]（[[1872年|1872]]-[[1888年]]（明治5-21年）に日本滞在）が測量・写真撮影{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1900年]]（明治33年）、[[濱田耕作]]による踏査・観察報告（最初の学術報告か）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）、敷地境界石設置{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1926年]]（大正15年）、測量図作成（帝室林野局、1928年（[[昭和]]3年）図化）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1934年]]（昭和9年）、[[梅原末治]]による調査報告{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）、前方部正面の周堤上の事務所改築に伴う発掘調査。外堤の検出（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1975年]]（昭和50年）、外構柵設置に先立つ発掘調査（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）、墳丘の一部崩落に伴う緊急調査（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、東側の周堤上の老人ホーム建設に伴う発掘調査。大溝の検出（藤井寺市教育委員会）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）、墳丘裾・外堤裾の護岸工事に先立つ発掘調査。中世期の山城利用に伴う大規模な改変の判明（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[2011年]]度（平成23年度）、測量図作成（百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界文化遺産登録推進本部会議）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:OkaMisanzai Kofun air.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|岡ミサンザイ古墳の航空写真
（1985年度）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[File:CG Oka Misanzai 3 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した岡ミサンザイ古墳}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 * 墳丘長：245メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成か。 ** 直径：150メートル ** 高さ：20メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成か。 ** 幅：180メートル ** 高さ：16.6メートル 上記の値は2011年度（平成23年度）作成の測量図によるもので、発掘調査で墳端を抑えることによる正確な数値ではない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘長は、古市古墳群では[[誉田御廟山古墳]]（羽曳野市誉田、[[応神天皇]]陵）・[[仲ツ山古墳|仲津山古墳]]（藤井寺市沢田、[[仲姫命]]陵）に次ぐ第3位、全国では第16位の規模になる。[[大仙陵古墳]]（[[堺市]][[堺区]]大仙町、[[仁徳天皇]]陵）の半分程度の値であるが、岡ミサンザイ古墳の築造当時（5世紀末頃）に比肩する他古墳はなく、同時代では突出する規模になる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 墳丘周囲に巡らされている周濠の幅は、主軸線上で約50メートルを測る{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。その外周には周堤が巡らされており、幅は約20メートルで、堤上には円筒埴輪列を有する{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。ただしこの周堤は、平面的には築造当初の企画を踏襲するものの、立面的には後世に0.6-2.5メートルの嵩上げがなされている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 また周堤の東側では、周堤に直交する形で大溝が検出されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。これは、周濠掘削の際に生じた湧水を段丘崖外へ排水する施設と見られ、古墳完成までには埋め戻しがなされている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。この大溝の発掘調査の際には、円筒埴輪のほか[[形象埴輪]]（家形・盾形・靫形・蓋形・人物埴輪）が出土している{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:CG Oka Misanzai 1 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。東方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部正面から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。西方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 5 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。南方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 7 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。南西方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 10 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == 被葬者 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+仲哀陵・雄略陵の陵名 ! ![[仲哀天皇]]
（第14代） ![[雄略天皇]]
（第21代） |- ! |[[File:Tennō Chūaii thumb.gif|100px]] |[[File:Emperor Yūryaku.jpg|100px]] |- ![[古事記]] |河内恵賀之長江 |河内之多治比高鸇 |- ![[日本書紀]] |長野陵 |丹比高鷲原陵 |- ![[延喜式]] |恵我長野西陵
（河内国志紀郡） |丹比高鷲原陵
（河内国丹比郡） |- !現在 |恵我長野西陵
（岡ミサンザイ古墳） |丹比高鷲原陵
（[[島泉丸山古墳]]・
島泉平塚古墳） |}
岡ミサンザイ古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第14代'''[[仲哀天皇]]'''の陵に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 402。。仲哀天皇の陵について、『[[古事記]]』『古事記』仲哀天皇段。では「河内恵賀之長江」の所在とあり、『[[日本書紀]]』『日本書紀』神功皇后摂政2年11月甲午（8日）条。では「長野陵」、『[[扶桑略記]]』では「長野山陵」とある{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|ミサンザイ古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では遠陵の「恵我長野西陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[志紀郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町で、陵戸1烟・守戸4烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|ミサンザイ古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 その後、陵の所在に関する所伝は喪失。元禄の探陵では上原村の塚山と沢田村の仲ツ山の候補が出て前者に定められたが{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}、幕末の修陵に際して[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）に岡ミサンザイ古墳に治定された{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。そして[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）-[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）に修陵がなされ、現在まで岡ミサンザイ古墳が仲哀天皇陵として踏襲されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 一方、近年では本古墳の真の被葬者として、第21代'''[[雄略天皇]]'''や、同天皇と同一視される'''[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]]'''（[[倭の五王]]の1人）に比定する説が有力視される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}[[足立倫行]] 「「倭の五王」をめぐる論点」『ここまでわかった! 「古代」謎の4世紀（新人物文庫315）』 『歴史読本』編集部編、KADOKAWA、2014年、pp. 48-61。『発見・検証 日本の古代II 騎馬文化と古代のイノベーション』 KADOKAWA、2016年、pp. 335-336。。雄略天皇の陵については、『古事記』『古事記』雄略天皇段。では「河内之多治比高鸇」の所在とあるほか、『日本書紀』『日本書紀』清寧天皇元年10月辛丑（9日）条。では「丹比高鷲原陵」、『延喜式』諸陵寮では遠陵の「丹比高鷲原陵」で河内国丹比郡の所在とする{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。同天皇に比定する説では、 :* [[稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣]]銘文（辛亥年<定説は[[471年]]>作）の「獲加多支鹵大王（ワカタケル大王）」 :* 『[[宋書]]』倭国伝の[[478年]]遣使記事の「倭王武」 のいずれも雄略天皇の和風諡号「オオハツセワカタケル（大泊瀬幼武/大長谷若建）」と対応することから、このワカタケル大王の実在を確実視し、その在位年と岡ミサンザイ古墳の築造時期も対応すると推測する{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。ただし、史書に見える仲哀天皇陵・雄略天皇陵の地名・所在郡の考証が詳らかでない点で、課題も残されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。また、5世紀には稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣銘文・江田船山古墳出土鉄刀銘文のように仮借が通例であって訓読みは確立していないとして、「武 = タケル」からの比定自体を批判する説もある河内春人 『倭の五王 -王位継承と五世紀の東アジア-（中公新書2470）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 163-206。。 なお現在の雄略天皇の陵は、北方の羽曳野市島泉8丁目に治定されているが、これは[[島泉丸山古墳]]・島泉平塚古墳という古墳2基をして前方後円形に整形したもので、元の古墳の規模も大王墓に見合うものではない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。またかつては雄略天皇の真陵を[[河内大塚山古墳]]（松原市西大塚・羽曳野市南恵我之荘）に比定する説も有力視されたが「雄略天皇」『日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版』 吉川弘文館、2010年。、近年では同古墳については古墳時代後期の築造とする説が定着している{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}川内眷三 {{PDFlink|[http://www.shitennoji.ac.jp/ibu/docs/toshokan/kiyou/57/kiyo57-02.pdf 「河内大塚山古墳の研究動向と周辺域古墳群の復原」]}} 『四天王寺大学紀要 第57号』 2014年、pp. 32-41。。 == 陪塚 == {{座標一覧|節="陪塚"}} [[File:Hachizuka Kofun (Fujiidera), zenkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鉢塚古墳 墳丘}}{{small|左に前方部、右奥に後円部。}}]] 本古墳の[[陪塚]]（陪冢）について、[[宮内庁]]による治定はない。 一方で考古学的には、岡ミサンザイ古墳付近にある次の1基が陪塚と推定されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 * '''鉢塚古墳'''（はちづかこふん） ** 所在地：藤井寺市藤井寺4丁目（{{Coord|34|34|4.75|N|135|35|43.20|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|name=鉢塚古墳}}） ** 形状：前方後円墳 ** 規模：墳丘長60メートル ** 史跡指定：国の史跡「鉢塚古墳」（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） *: 岡ミサンザイ古墳の後円部側北方に位置する{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。小型の前方後円墳で、前方部を西方に向ける。葺石は認められていない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘周囲には周濠が認められる（現在は埋没）。埋葬施設や、副葬品については不明。出土円筒埴輪から岡ミサンザイ古墳と同時期の築造と推定され、岡ミサンザイ古墳の陪塚に比定される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 また、岡ミサンザイ古墳の前方部正面に位置した落塚古墳（現在は消滅）を陪塚とする説もある[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/kunifusiteibunkazai/1387696968186.html 鉢塚古墳と割塚古墳]（藤井寺市ホームページ）。。こちらは直径20メートルの[[円墳]]であるが、[[1965年]]（昭和40年）頃に調査なく消滅したため、様相は詳らかでない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 周辺では、岡ミサンザイ古墳の前方部東隅角側に割塚古墳（国の史跡）と[[岡古墳]]（現在は消滅）が認められているが、これらは岡ミサンザイ古墳から約1世紀遡る時期の古墳になる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。そのほかに1877年（明治10年）頃の[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]の記録では、上記4基のほかに3基を加えた計7基の存在が記載されているが、現在ではそれらの詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。いずれにしても、前時代の[[市ノ山古墳|市野山古墳]]の段階で陪塚が10基以上であるのに比して、岡ミサンザイ古墳の段階では陪塚の数が激減する点が注目される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 == 後世の城郭化 == 岡ミサンザイ古墳は、[[中世]]期から城郭利用されたことが知られる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。近年の城郭考古学では、測量図を基に、後円部が本丸、前方部が櫓台とされたほか、前方部先端に横堀が切られて馬出しが形成され、さらに数本の竪堀が切られて、古墳全域が城郭化された様子が指摘される[[千田嘉博]] 「城郭化された巨大古墳が語る大敵を叩く絶妙の策」『歴史街道 2017年1月号』 PHP研究所、2016年、pp. 78-82。。また、一帯では特に[[大坂の陣|大坂夏の陣]]における[[道明寺の戦い|道明寺・誉田の戦い]]が知られることから、その戦いの際に古市古墳群西端の岡ミサンザイ古墳に豊臣方の拠点が置かれ、東方から来る徳川方に対して東側の古墳群列が防衛線として想定されたとする説が挙げられている。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺4丁目 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[近鉄南大阪線|南大阪線]] [[藤井寺駅]] （徒歩約15分） '''周辺''' * [[アイセルシュラホール]]（藤井寺市立生涯学習センター） - 藤井寺市域の古市古墳群の出土品等を展示。 * [[葛井寺]] - [[西国三十三所]]第五番札所。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 仲哀天皇陵（岡ミサンザイ）古墳 史跡説明板（藤井寺市、2016年設置） * 鉢塚古墳 史跡説明板（藤井寺市、2016年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=仲哀天皇陵古墳（藤井寺市文化財報告 第36集 -古市古墳群の調査研究報告V-）|publisher=藤井寺市教育委員会事務局|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[笹山晴生]] 「仲哀天皇」|ref={{Harvid|仲哀天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔 「恵我長野西陵」（仲哀天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=ミサンザイ古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|ミサンザイ古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=天野末喜|year=1989|chapter=仲哀天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B2%A1%E3%83%9F%E3%82%B5%E3%83%B3%E3%82%B6%E3%82%A4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-1443489 岡ミサンザイ古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|岡ミサンザイ古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1975|chapter=仲哀天皇陵前藤井寺部事務所改築敷地の調査|title=書陵部紀要 第26号|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1977|chapter=仲哀天皇陵外構柵設置区域の事前調査|title=書陵部紀要 第28号|publisher=宮内庁書陵部|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=恵我長野西陵墳丘崩壊部露出遺構の調査及び崩壊部応急保護工事箇所の調査|title=書陵部紀要 第36号|publisher=宮内庁書陵部|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite 第50号|publisher=宮内庁書陵部|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=仲哀天皇 恵我長野西陵汚水桝取設箇所の調査|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo052b020.pdf 書陵部紀要 revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[岡ミサンザイ古墳]] to [[Okamisanzai Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 岡ミサンザイ古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属 = [[古市古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺4丁目 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 55.35 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 35|経度秒 = 38.60 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長245m
高さ20m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末葉 |埋葬施設 = 不明（一説に[[横穴式石室]]） |陪塚 = 1基 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第14代[[仲哀天皇]]
（一説）第21代[[雄略天皇]]
（一説）[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]] |出土品 = [[円筒埴輪]]・形象埴輪 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「恵我長野西陵」 |特記事項 = 全国第16位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |地図 = Osaka city |ラベル = 岡
ミサンザイ
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南西 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, haisho-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[仲哀天皇]]恵我長野西陵 拝所}}]] '''岡ミサンザイ古墳'''（おかみさんざいこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''恵我長野西陵'''（えがのながののにしのみささぎ、惠我長野西陵）」として第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 全国では第16位の規模の古墳で、[[5世紀]]末葉（[[古墳時代]]中期）頃の築造と推定される。考古学には第21代[[雄略天皇]]、[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]]（[[倭の五王]]の1人）の真陵とする説が有力視される。 == 概要 == 大阪府東部、藤井寺市・[[羽曳野市]]・[[松原市]]にまたがる大古墳群である[[古市古墳群]]のうち、西端の位置に築造された巨大前方後円墳である。古墳名の「岡」は地名で、「ミサンザイ」は「ミササギ（陵）」の転訛。墳丘は[[中世]]の山城築城、および幕末の修陵の際に大きく改変を受けている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の[[仲哀天皇]]陵として同庁の管理下にあるが、これまでに墳丘裾部や古墳周辺部において[[宮内庁書陵部]]や大阪府教育委員会・藤井寺市教育委員会による数次の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南南西方に向ける{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は元は3段築成と見られるが、修陵の際に5段に改変されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘外表では[[埴輪]]片が検出されている一方、[[葺石]]は認められていない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘くびれ部東側には[[造出]]を有する（西側造出の存在有無は不明）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘周囲には幅広の周濠（主軸線上で幅50メートル）が巡らされるほか、その外側には埴輪列を有する周堤（幅20メートル）が巡らされる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、墳丘形状から[[横穴式石室]]である可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。副葬品は不明{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。また周辺では、後円部北側に[[陪塚]]と見られる鉢塚古墳（国の[[史跡]]）が築造されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 この岡ミサンザイ古墳は、出土埴輪より[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。築造当時としては全国で突出する規模であり、葺石の停止および横穴式石室の新規導入（推定）の点で、それまでの古墳とは一線を画す様相の古墳になる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、前述のように現在は宮内庁により第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の陵に治定されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。ただし仲哀天皇は非実在性が強く、築造年代も仲哀天皇の想定年代に合わないことから、現在では第21代[[雄略天皇]]や、同天皇と同一視される[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]]（『[[宋書]]』倭国伝）・ワカタケル大王（[[稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣]]銘）の陵とする説が有力視される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）、[[戸田忠至|戸田大和守]]により[[仲哀天皇]]の陵に治定{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）-[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）、修陵{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[明治]]初年、墳丘上の雑木林（クヌギなど）の伐採、マツ・スギの植林{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * 明治期、宮内省（現・[[宮内庁]]）により仲哀天皇陵に治定。 * 明治期、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]（[[1872年|1872]]-[[1888年]]（明治5-21年）に日本滞在）が測量・写真撮影{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1900年]]（明治33年）、[[濱田耕作]]による踏査・観察報告（最初の学術報告か）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）、敷地境界石設置{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1926年]]（大正15年）、測量図作成（帝室林野局、1928年（[[昭和]]3年）図化）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1934年]]（昭和9年）、[[梅原末治]]による調査報告{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）、前方部正面の周堤上の事務所改築に伴う発掘調査。外堤の検出（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1975年]]（昭和50年）、外構柵設置に先立つ発掘調査（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）、墳丘の一部崩落に伴う緊急調査（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、東側の周堤上の老人ホーム建設に伴う発掘調査。大溝の検出（藤井寺市教育委員会）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）、墳丘裾・外堤裾の護岸工事に先立つ発掘調査。中世期の山城利用に伴う大規模な改変の判明（宮内庁書陵部）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 * [[2011年]]度（平成23年度）、測量図作成（百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界文化遺産登録推進本部会議）{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 == 墳丘 == [[File:OkaMisanzai Kofun air.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|岡ミサンザイ古墳の航空写真
（1985年度）}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[File:CG Oka Misanzai 3 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した岡ミサンザイ古墳}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 * 墳丘長：245メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成か。 ** 直径：150メートル ** 高さ：20メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成か。 ** 幅：180メートル ** 高さ：16.6メートル 上記の値は2011年度（平成23年度）作成の測量図によるもので、発掘調査で墳端を抑えることによる正確な数値ではない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘長は、古市古墳群では[[誉田御廟山古墳]]（羽曳野市誉田、[[応神天皇]]陵）・[[仲ツ山古墳|仲津山古墳]]（藤井寺市沢田、[[仲姫命]]陵）に次ぐ第3位、全国では第16位の規模になる。[[大仙陵古墳]]（[[堺市]][[堺区]]大仙町、[[仁徳天皇]]陵）の半分程度の値であるが、岡ミサンザイ古墳の築造当時（5世紀末頃）に比肩する他古墳はなく、同時代では突出する規模になる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 墳丘周囲に巡らされている周濠の幅は、主軸線上で約50メートルを測る{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。その外周には周堤が巡らされており、幅は約20メートルで、堤上には円筒埴輪列を有する{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。ただしこの周堤は、平面的には築造当初の企画を踏襲するものの、立面的には後世に0.6-2.5メートルの嵩上げがなされている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 また周堤の東側では、周堤に直交する形で大溝が検出されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。これは、周濠掘削の際に生じた湧水を段丘崖外へ排水する施設と見られ、古墳完成までには埋め戻しがなされている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。この大溝の発掘調査の際には、円筒埴輪のほか[[形象埴輪]]（家形・盾形・靫形・蓋形・人物埴輪）が出土している{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 File:CG Oka Misanzai 1 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。東方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部正面から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。西方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 5 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。南方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 7 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。南西方向から見る File:CG Oka Misanzai 10 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == 被葬者 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+仲哀陵・雄略陵の陵名 ! ![[仲哀天皇]]
（第14代） ![[雄略天皇]]
（第21代） |- ! |[[File:Tennō Chūaii thumb.gif|100px]] |[[File:Emperor Yūryaku.jpg|100px]] |- ![[古事記]] |河内恵賀之長江 |河内之多治比高鸇 |- ![[日本書紀]] |長野陵 |丹比高鷲原陵 |- ![[延喜式]] |恵我長野西陵
（河内国志紀郡） |丹比高鷲原陵
（河内国丹比郡） |- !現在 |恵我長野西陵
（岡ミサンザイ古墳） |丹比高鷲原陵
（[[島泉丸山古墳]]・
島泉平塚古墳） |}
岡ミサンザイ古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第14代'''[[仲哀天皇]]'''の陵に治定している[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014年、p. 402。。仲哀天皇の陵について、『[[古事記]]』『古事記』仲哀天皇段。では「河内恵賀之長江」の所在とあり、『[[日本書紀]]』『日本書紀』神功皇后摂政2年11月甲午（8日）条。では「長野陵」、『[[扶桑略記]]』では「長野山陵」とある{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|ミサンザイ古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では遠陵の「恵我長野西陵」として記載され、[[河内国]][[志紀郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町で、陵戸1烟・守戸4烟を毎年あてるとする{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|ミサンザイ古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 その後、陵の所在に関する所伝は喪失。元禄の探陵では上原村の塚山と沢田村の仲ツ山の候補が出て前者に定められたが{{Sfn|恵我長野西陵（国史）}}、幕末の修陵に際して[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）に岡ミサンザイ古墳に治定された{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。そして[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）-[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）に修陵がなされ、現在まで岡ミサンザイ古墳が仲哀天皇陵として踏襲されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=1-35}}。 一方、近年では本古墳の真の被葬者として、第21代'''[[雄略天皇]]'''や、同天皇と同一視される'''[[武 (倭王)|倭王武]]'''（[[倭の五王]]の1人）に比定する説が有力視される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}[[足立倫行]] 「「倭の五王」をめぐる論点」『ここまでわかった! 「古代」謎の4世紀（新人物文庫315）』 『歴史読本』編集部編、KADOKAWA、2014年、pp. 48-61。『発見・検証 日本の古代II 騎馬文化と古代のイノベーション』 KADOKAWA、2016年、pp. 335-336。。雄略天皇の陵については、『古事記』『古事記』雄略天皇段。では「河内之多治比高鸇」の所在とあるほか、『日本書紀』『日本書紀』清寧天皇元年10月辛丑（9日）条。では「丹比高鷲原陵」、『延喜式』諸陵寮では遠陵の「丹比高鷲原陵」で河内国丹比郡の所在とする{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。同天皇に比定する説では、 :* [[稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣]]銘文（辛亥年<定説は[[471年]]>作）の「獲加多支鹵大王（ワカタケル大王）」 :* 『[[宋書]]』倭国伝の[[478年]]遣使記事の「倭王武」 のいずれも雄略天皇の和風諡号「オオハツセワカタケル（大泊瀬幼武/大長谷若建）」と対応することから、このワカタケル大王の実在を確実視し、その在位年と岡ミサンザイ古墳の築造時期も対応すると推測する{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。ただし、史書に見える仲哀天皇陵・雄略天皇陵の地名・所在郡の考証が詳らかでない点で、課題も残されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。また、5世紀には稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣銘文・江田船山古墳出土鉄刀銘文のように仮借が通例であって訓読みは確立していないとして、「武 = タケル」からの比定自体を批判する説もある河内春人 『倭の五王 -王位継承と五世紀の東アジア-（中公新書2470）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 163-206。。 なお現在の雄略天皇の陵は、北方の羽曳野市島泉8丁目に治定されているが、これは[[島泉丸山古墳]]・島泉平塚古墳という古墳2基をして前方後円形に整形したもので、元の古墳の規模も大王墓に見合うものではない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。またかつては雄略天皇の真陵を[[河内大塚山古墳]]（松原市西大塚・羽曳野市南恵我之荘）に比定する説も有力視されたが「雄略天皇」『日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版』 吉川弘文館、2010年。、近年では同古墳については古墳時代後期の築造とする説が定着している{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}川内眷三 {{PDFlink|[http://www.shitennoji.ac.jp/ibu/docs/toshokan/kiyou/57/kiyo57-02.pdf 「河内大塚山古墳の研究動向と周辺域古墳群の復原」]}} 『四天王寺大学紀要 第57号』 2014年、pp. 32-41。。 == 陪塚 == {{座標一覧|節="陪塚"}} [[File:Hachizuka Kofun (Fujiidera), zenkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鉢塚古墳 墳丘}}{{small|左に前方部、右奥に後円部。}}]] 本古墳の[[陪塚]]（陪冢）について、[[宮内庁]]による治定はない。 一方で考古学的には、岡ミサンザイ古墳付近にある次の1基が陪塚と推定されている{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 * '''鉢塚古墳'''（はちづかこふん） ** 所在地：藤井寺市藤井寺4丁目（{{Coord|34|34|4.75|N|135|35|43.20|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|name=鉢塚古墳}}） ** 形状：前方後円墳 ** 規模：墳丘長60メートル ** 史跡指定：国の史跡「鉢塚古墳」（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） *: 岡ミサンザイ古墳の後円部側北方に位置する{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。小型の前方後円墳で、前方部を西方に向ける。葺石は認められていない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。墳丘周囲には周濠が認められる（現在は埋没）。埋葬施設や、副葬品については不明。出土円筒埴輪から岡ミサンザイ古墳と同時期の築造と推定され、岡ミサンザイ古墳の陪塚に比定される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 また、岡ミサンザイ古墳の前方部正面に位置した落塚古墳（現在は消滅）を陪塚とする説もある[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/kunifusiteibunkazai/1387696968186.html 鉢塚古墳と割塚古墳]（藤井寺市ホームページ）。。こちらは直径20メートルの[[円墳]]であるが、[[1965年]]（昭和40年）頃に調査なく消滅したため、様相は詳らかでない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 周辺では、岡ミサンザイ古墳の前方部東隅角側に割塚古墳（国の史跡）と[[岡古墳]]（現在は消滅）が認められているが、これらは岡ミサンザイ古墳から約1世紀遡る時期の古墳になる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。そのほかに1877年（明治10年）頃の[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]の記録では、上記4基のほかに3基を加えた計7基の存在が記載されているが、現在ではそれらの詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。いずれにしても、前時代の[[市ノ山古墳|市野山古墳]]の段階で陪塚が10基以上であるのに比して、岡ミサンザイ古墳の段階では陪塚の数が激減する点が注目される{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。 == 後世の城郭化 == 岡ミサンザイ古墳は、[[中世]]期から城郭利用されたことが知られる{{Sfn|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}}。近年の城郭考古学では、測量図を基に、後円部が本丸、前方部が櫓台とされたほか、前方部先端に横堀が切られて馬出しが形成され、さらに数本の竪堀が切られて、古墳全域が城郭化された様子が指摘される[[千田嘉博]] 「城郭化された巨大古墳が語る大敵を叩く絶妙の策」『歴史街道 2017年1月号』 PHP研究所、2016年、pp. 78-82。。また、一帯では特に[[大坂の陣|大坂夏の陣]]における[[道明寺の戦い|道明寺・誉田の戦い]]が知られることから、その戦いの際に古市古墳群西端の岡ミサンザイ古墳に豊臣方の拠点が置かれ、東方から来る徳川方に対して東側の古墳群列が防衛線として想定されたとする説が挙げられている。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺4丁目 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[近畿日本鉄道]]（近鉄）[[近鉄南大阪線|南大阪線]] [[藤井寺駅]] （徒歩約15分） '''周辺''' * [[アイセルシュラホール]]（藤井寺市立生涯学習センター） - 藤井寺市域の古市古墳群の出土品等を展示。 * [[葛井寺]] - [[西国三十三所]]第五番札所。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Okamisanzai Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historic sites]] [[Category:Imperial tombs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:kamisanzai Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|version=99994837|comment=Imported full ja history}}

A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|12=draft}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|12=draft}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=draft}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=draft}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=draft|12=draft}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fujidera 4-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 55.35 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 38.60 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |notes = 1 |Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, figurative haniwa |Mausoleum = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|version=99994837|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Okamisanzai Kofun]] [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:kamisanzai Kofun]]

Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) |notes = 1 |Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Bu of Wa|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa |Mausoleum = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bu of Wa|qq=}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū|qq=}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Yamato no Kami|qq=}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genji|qq=}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|qq=}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bu of Wa|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = [[File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]

Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) |notes = 1 |Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Bu of Wa|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa |Mausoleum = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bu of Wa|qq=}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū|qq=}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Yamato no Kami|qq=}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genji|qq=}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|qq=}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bu of Wa|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Full view of the mound (right: front section, left rear: circular rear section) |part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}}, Fujidera 4-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 33|coordinates = 55.35 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 38.60 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) |notes = 1 |Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|King Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/King Bu of Wa|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa |Mausoleum = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bu of Wa|qq=}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū|qq=}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Yamato no Kami|qq=}}. In {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genji|qq=}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|qq=}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|ja|武 (倭王)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bu of Wa|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) and one of the Kofuns comprising the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], but no evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Yamato no Kami|qq=}}. In [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1 (1864)-[[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|qq=}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles ==
