[[co:commons:Category:Oka Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Oka Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Oka Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Oka Misanzai Kofun |image = File:Oka Misanzai Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg |part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} |location = {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|1=Fujiidera}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.5658|N|135.5942|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length: 245m
Height: 20m (circular rear section) |built = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |burial = Unknown (possibly {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) |notes = 1 |Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}
(Alternative theory) {{ill|Bu of Wa|en|Bu of Wa|WD=Q28692301|lt=King Bu of Wa|1=Bu of Wa}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa |Mausoleum = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}; {{ill|Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|en|Bu of Wa|WD=Q28692301|lt=Bu of Wa|1=Bu of Wa}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Yamato no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|en|Bu of Wa|WD=Q28692301|lt=Bu of Wa|1=Bu of Wa}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|version=99994837|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] {{Commons category|Oka Misanzai Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11471921}} (Alternative theory) {{ill|Bu of Wa|en|Bu of Wa|WD=Q28692301|lt=King Bu of Wa|1=Bu of Wa}} |burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa |Mausoleum = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation: "Ega Nagano Nishi no Misasagi" (恵我長野西陵) |notes = Ranked 16th largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun area Ranking (Nationwide)] (Sakai City Homepage, updated May 13, 2018). |map = Osaka city |map_dot_label = Oka
Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}; {{ill|Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|en|Bu of Wa|WD=Q28692301|lt=Bu of Wa|1=Bu of Wa}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Yamato no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} * {{ill|Bu of Wa|en|Bu of Wa|WD=Q28692301|lt=Bu of Wa|1=Bu of Wa}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|岡ミサンザイ古墳|version=99994837|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:岡ミサンザイ古墳]] {{Commons category|Oka Misanzai Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11471921}} Misanzai
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |commemorates = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}; {{ill|Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} }} '''Oka Misanzai Kofun''' (岡ミサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Fujidera, Osaka Prefecture. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}) and one of the Kofuns comprising the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a World Historic Site site. Although the identity of the individual buried within the kofun is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designation1 it as the tomb of the 14th emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}''', naming it the "Ega no Nagano no Nishi no Misasagi" (惠我長野西陵). Nationally, this kofun is the 16th largest Kofun.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ancient Tomb area Ranking (National)] (Sakai City official website, updated May 13, 2018). Archaeological evidence suggests that it was constructed in the late 5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Many researchers now propose that the burial site is the true tomb of the 21st emperor, '''{{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}''', or of {{ill|Bu of Wa|en|Bu of Wa|WD=Q28692301|lt=Bu of Wa|1=Bu of Wa}}. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the Furuichi Kofun Group in eastern Osaka Prefecture, Oka Misanzai Kofun is a massive keyhole-shaped mound. The name "Oka" derives from the local area, while "Misanzai" is a corruption of the term "Misasagi" (imperial tomb). The mound has undergone significant modifications due to the construction of a medieval fortress and repairs made during the late Edo period.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳|2014|pp=111-124}} Currently, the site is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as Emperor Chūai’s mausoleum. The mound is oriented with the square front facing south-southwest.{{Harvid|仲哀天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} Originally constructed in three tiers, the mound was altered to a five-tier structure during restoration. The outer layer of the mound contains fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, but no evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} has been found. A platform on the eastern side of the constriction (with the presence of a corresponding western platform uncertain) has also been identified. A wide moat (50 meters across along the main axis) encircles the mound, with an earthen embankment (20 meters wide) on its outer perimeter, adorned with rows of cylindrical haniwa. Based on the type of the mound, the burial chamber is speculated to be a {{ill|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Haniwa fragments suggest that the mound was constructed in the late 5th century, a period marked by a shift from earlier kofun traditions, as reflected in the cessation of fukiishi and the adoption of the yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == Historical Background == In {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3 (1863), the mound was designation1 as Emperor Chūai’s tomb by {{ill|Toda Yamato no Kami|ja|戸田大和守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Yamato no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|WD=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 1 (1864)-{{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 1 (1865), restoration work was undertaken. However, modern research questions the attribution to Emperor Chūai, with several scholars favoring Emperor Yūryaku or Wō (King of Wa) based on historical and archaeological evidence. The site has also been associated with medieval fortifications and played a potential role during the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|lt=Summer Campaign of the Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}}, where it might have served as a strategic base.{{Harvid|千田2017th year }} == burial == No definitive burial chamber has been excavated, but the mound is hypothesized to contain a yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu. == area == The mound's area are as follows: * Total length: 245 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 150 meters ** Height: 20 meters * Front square section: ** Width: 180 meters ** Height: 16.6 meters This makes Oka Misanzai Kofun the third-largest mound in the Furuichi Kofun Group and the 16th-largest nationwide. == Surrounding Tombs == Nearby, the {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun|qq=}}, located to the north of the mound's rear section, is considered a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mound). == Modern Access == '''location''' * Fujidera 4-chome, Fujidera City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Fujidera Station on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor
